The sentence states that Ms. Jackson lent me some sugar (Lm), and neither Janet nor Latoya did, meaning it is not the case that Janet lent it or Latoya lent it. This is a conjunction of Lm and the negation of the disjunction (Lj ∨ Ll). Therefore, the correct translation is Lm • ~(Lj ∨ Ll), which matches option C.

\boxed{C}
